title: bumelia ferruginea
intro: Bumelia ferruginea, also known as the rusty-barked bumelia or rusty-barked dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots have been employed to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its high content of tannins and phenolic compounds. Bumelia ferruginea can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
